name: Clotted Cream
recipeInstructions: Preferably extra-rich milk, if available. Choose a wide-mouthed bowl or stainless steel bowl with sloping sides. Fill it with milk, leaving enough of a rim free to avoid spillage. Add 20 fluid ounces double cream. Leave in the refrigerator for at least several hours, and preferably overnight. Set the bowl over a pan of water kept at 82 degrees C (180 F) until the top of the milk is crusted with a nubbly yellowish-cream surface. This will take at least 1 1/2 hours, but allow much longer to ensure it forms properly. Remove the bowl from the pan and cool it rapidly in a bowl of ice water. Store in the refrigerator until very cold. Scrape off the crust with a skimmer and place into another bowl with some of the creamy liquid underneath; the clotted part firms up significantly, needing the liquid. The milk left over makes delicious rice pudding or can be used in baking, especially yeast buns.
